summary: #50093 remains the live canonical issue for WhatsApp reconnect missed-message backfill. #76175 and #80642 are related, useful, open fix candidates, but neither is merge-ready: both are blocked on real behavior proof, #76175 also has dependency-guard failures, and the two PRs represent different product choices.

actions.reason: Keep #50093 as the canonical open tracker for the unresolved WhatsApp reconnect missed-message backfill problem.
Keep #76175 open as a related candidate fix. It is useful but blocked by proof/check gates and by the maintainer product decision about whether reconnect catch-up should be opt-in configuration.
Keep #80642 open as a related candidate fix. It is narrower than #76175 but still draft and blocked by real behavior proof and maintainer choice between competing repair approaches.
Maintainers need to choose the canonical repair direction for #50093 and define the real WhatsApp proof bar before either PR should become the merge path.

needs_human: Choose the canonical repair direction for #50093: config-gated offline-message processing in #76175, default reconnect catch-up widening in #80642, or a combined design after real WhatsApp proof is supplied.
